name: Xero
description: Bookkeeping operations against a Xero organisation through Eden's brokered-capability framework: the agent's container never holds any Xero credential. Eden keeps the OAuth grant and executes only a whitelisted set of operations — read reference data, search bills, look up and create contacts, and create DRAFT bills (a human approves them in Xero) with attached source files. Which operation groups are enabled is your choice at install time and editable any time from the Deployment tab, with instant effect.
setup:
**Operator setup (one-time, per Eden installation):** register an Eden-owned app at [developer.xero.com](https://developer.xero.com/app/manage) — *New app*, integration type **Web app**, redirect URI `<origin>/connections/xero/callback` (your Eden control plane's public origin). Copy the client id and secret into the control plane's environment as `EDEN_XERO_CLIENT_ID` / `EDEN_XERO_CLIENT_SECRET` and restart Eden. No per-agent app is needed.

**Connect:** after installing, open the agent's **Deployment** tab and click **Connect Xero** on the Connections card. One consent screen authorises the fixed scope set above; if the Xero login can reach more than one organisation, a picker asks which one this agent works in (exactly one binds automatically). You can re-pick by reconnecting.

**How credentials flow:** the OAuth grant — Xero rotates its refresh token on every refresh — never leaves the Eden control plane, and no `XERO_*` variable is ever injected into the agent's container. Each shipped tool POSTs its typed input to Eden (`/api/capabilities/xero/...`) with the deployment's `EDEN_TEAM_TOKEN`; Eden validates the request server-side (bills are always created as DRAFT `ACCPAY`, account codes and currencies must exist in the organisation, attachments are capped at 10 MiB) and performs the one whitelisted operation itself. Anything not in the whitelist — authorising, paying, voiding, deleting, payroll — does not exist through Eden, regardless of what the consented scopes would allow.

**Operations selection:** the install wizard's Operations card chooses which operation groups are enabled (read groups default on; the write groups — *Create contacts*, *Create draft bills* — are opt-in). Eden enforces the selection on every call, so changing it later from the Deployment tab takes effect at the agent's next call — no reconnect, no redeploy.

**Audit:** every capability call — allowed or refused — is logged control-plane-side with a redacted input summary (`capability_calls`).
